lesson.subtitle: Aula 12 -- Midpoint Review -- Integration Check
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Natalie Viegas | Aula 12 -- Midpoint Review

listenings.text: Hi Natalie, this is Daniel from the leadership team. I wanted to send you a quick midpoint update on the cloud project. Things are going well. Last quarter we delivered the new platform, and the client was very satisfied. Right now, the team is preparing the next report, which is due on Friday. The proposal has already been approved by procurement, so we are clear to move forward. If we keep this pace, we will hit our target before the end of the year. In the next phase, results should be even stronger than last quarter, because the most reliable vendors are now on board. I think you have built real credibility with the client, and that is the most valuable asset we have. Let us talk on Thursday, and please prepare a short summary of your progress so far. Thanks, and great work.

Hi, this is Natalie, recording my midpoint self-assessment. When I started this program, I could only introduce myself and talk about my routine. Today I can lead a meeting, write a clear report, present to leadership, and negotiate with a client. Last month I delivered a pitch in English, and the audience really understood my message. I have already presented data, given feedback, and built rapport with new contacts. The grammar is becoming natural: I use the present perfect to talk about experience, the passive voice in my reports, and the first conditional to discuss plans. I still make small mistakes with question tags and comparatives, so I will keep practicing those. If I continue at this pace, I will be fluent by the end of the year. I am proud of my progress, and I feel more confident in every meeting.

extra_audio.text: First, you set a clear objective for the meeting. Then you present the data, comparing this quarter with the last one. After that, you give honest feedback and listen to the other person. Next, you agree on the next steps and confirm them with a question tag. Finally, you reach out to follow up and nurture the relationship over time.
